title: State peer-set tiers
description: Reference taxonomy mapping ECI state/UT codes into peer sets used by IndicatorRanked / IndicatorChoropleth filters. A state may belong to multiple tiers (Sikkim is both `special_category` and `neh`); tiers are NOT a partition. Tier identity is defined by what cites it (different policy documents use different groupings) — set equality is not a reason to merge. The catalogue (`topic-catalogue.json`) is the binding contract for which tier IDs may be used as `peer_set_default`; this file binds membership to those IDs. See ADR-0022 §peer-sets and `docs/concepts/peer-sets.md`.

x-changelog.description: Initial schema. Tier-keyed with rich tier objects (id, label, definition_kind, definition, optional authority + authority_url, members[]). Hand-authored taxonomy per panel decision 2026-05-13 (Hohpe + Governance Strategist + Data Architect personas); empty `sources[]` at file level because tier classifications are constitutional/statutory FACTS, not fetched artifacts (legal citations live in per-tier `authority` instead). Membership overlap is allowed by construction; UT tiers must partition. See TODO/IA-RESET-PLACE-FIRST-WITH-TOPIC-FRONT-DOOR.md P3.1.

sources.description: Per CLAUDE.md §12 / ADR-0002. For this file the canonical shape is `[]` (hand-authored) — tier classifications are constitutional facts (Articles 371, 239, 239AA), statutory facts (NEHC Act 1971, FC reports), or editorial residuals; we did not 'fetch' them. Per-tier `authority` carries the legal citation.

tiers.description: Open list of tiers. Adding a new tier is additive (no schema bump here); broadening the catalogue's `peer_set_default` enum to reference it is a minor bump on `topic-catalogue.schema.json`.

id.description: Stable identifier. Referenced by `topic-catalogue.json` artifacts' `peer_set_default`. Renames cascade and are major bumps.

label.description: Short human-facing label, shown next to the filter chip.

definition_kind.description: What KIND of authority defines this tier. Drives provenance discipline: constitutional/statutory tiers carry an `authority`; editorial/residual tiers may not.

definition.description: One-paragraph definition explaining what makes a state a member. Shown as tooltip / methodology copy.

authority.description: Free-text legal citation (e.g. 'Article 371-A; NEHC Act 1971'). Required by convention for constitutional/statutory/fc_derived tiers; omitted for editorial/residual tiers.

authority_url.description: Optional URL pointing at the authority (e.g. India Code page for an Article, fincomindia.nic.in for an FC report). Recon required — never invent.

members.description: ECI state/UT codes that belong to this tier. May be empty for `research` tiers awaiting recon (e.g. FC quintile bins) — populated in a follow-up.

notes.description: Maintainer notes (recon TODOs, refresh cadence reminders, dual-membership rationale).
